name: California Pharmacist License
description: Become a pharmacist in California. $250 application fee, NAPLEX and CPJE required. Steps, fees, and board contact info.

provider.name: California Board of Pharmacy
provider.parentOrganization: Title Protection Only (Business and Professions Code Section 2585)

address.description: 1625 N Market Blvd, Suite S200, Sacramento, CA 95834-1929

requiredDocuments.name: Exam score reports
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and CPJE.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a California pharmacist license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The California Board of Pharmacy charges a $250 application fee for new pharmacist applicants.

mainEntity.name: What exams are required for a California pharmacist license?

acceptedAnswer.text: California requires both the NAPLEX and the CPJE. The CPJE is California's own jurisprudence and practice standards exam. Schedule it early so it does not delay your license.

mainEntity.name: Does California have reciprocity for pharmacists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in California. To work in California, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to get a pharmacist license in California?

acceptedAnswer.text: Processing typically takes 4 to 6 weeks once all documents and fingerprints have been received by the Board.

mainEntity.name: What usually slows down a California pharmacist application?

acceptedAnswer.text: Out-of-state fingerprinting can add 2-4 weeks. Missing or incomplete documents, exam timing (especially scheduling the CPJE), and name mismatches across documents are also common causes of delays.
